level_signals: 5+ years of experience on growth, product, or operations, or as a SWE
Base salary range $180,800–$226,000 consistent with Senior IC
Presents to executive team; owns strategy and analytics independently

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience on growth, product, or operations, or as a SWE.
at minimum the role requires the ability to do data analytics using SQL or Python
Strong technical background (a degree in STEM is ideal, and at minimum the role requires the ability to do data analytics using SQL or Python).
Prior experience with operations-heavy business models (e.g. on-demand, marketplace, logistics, supply chain)
Excellent problem-solving capabilities and a robust analytical approach, with the ability to leverage data to inform growth strategies
An entrepreneurial spirit, comfortable with navigating ambiguity and scaling new products or services from the ground up.
Prior experience building with AI tools and workflows - you've tinkered, shipped something, and can quickly pick up new AI tooling as the landscape evolves

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: experience working on operational challenges or as a consultant is a plus

scope.surface: Outlier expert contributor marketplace — supply-side growth engine for Generative AI projects
scope.business_model: B2B AI data/services marketplace; supply-side marketplace for expert contributors (PhDs, coders, domain experts)
primary_metrics_inferred: Expert contributor acquisition and activation rates
Project ramp success (qualified experts meeting delivery timelines)
core_responsibilities: Drive critical growth projects that expand and improve the expert supply available for Scale's highest-priority AI projects

Develop and enhance growth strategies, funnels, and pipelines for acquiring expert contributors through channels such as outbound, referrals, hackathons, partnerships, paid acquisition, and professional communities
Improve onboarding, screening, verification, and training systems so that experts can be evaluated, activated, and ramped onto customer projects quickly and reliably
Manage supply-side operations across the contributor lifecycle, including activation, retention, churn mitigation, skill/profile validation, and routing contributors to the most relevant projects
Build analytical systems and operating rhythms to forecast capacity needs, diagnose funnel bottlenecks, and ensure projects ramp enough qualified experts to meet delivery timelines
Present regular progress updates to Scale's executive team, translating supply health, ramp progress, and growth initiatives into clear recommendations and decisions

compensation.equity_form: equity grant (subject to Board of Director approval)

green_flags: Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Clear and specific scope — supply-side growth for AI expert marketplace (Outlier)
Role has direct executive visibility (regular progress updates to executive team)
Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, vision, learning stipend, and generous PTO
Company has well-known enterprise and government customers (Meta, U.S. Army, Air Force, etc.)
red_flags: All requirements listed under 'Ideally, you'd have' — language suggests preferred, but context and must-have signals (SQL/Python explicitly called 'at minimum') create ambiguity about true hard requirements
No explicit mention of visa sponsorship — unclear for non-US candidates
90-day waiting period policy before reconsidering candidates for the same role may deter applicants who miss the first round
Role locations limited to SF, NYC, Seattle — no remote option stated, eliminating distributed candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + responsibility scope + years required)

ambiguities: All requirements appear under 'Ideally, you'd have:' which could imply all are preferred/nice-to-have; however, SQL/Python analytics is explicitly called 'at minimum the role requires', making it a hard requirement. Other items were classified as hard based on overall role criticality language and unconditional framing.

STEM degree is described as 'ideal' but SQL/Python ability is stated as 'at minimum' — creates ambiguity about whether a non-STEM degree with strong analytics skills would suffice. Treated as hard but with accepts_equivalent_experience flagged.
Location type: JD provides pay transparency for SF, NYC, Seattle only, implying onsite; no remote or hybrid language used. Treated as onsite but unconfirmed.
